title: Understanding Linear Momentum
text: Linear momentum is calculated using the formula: momentum $$=$$ mass * velocity. To find Earth's linear momentum, we need to know its mass and velocity.

title: Finding Earth's Velocity
text: Earth's velocity in its orbit can be calculated using the formula: velocity $$=$$ $$\frac{2\pi radius}{time}$$ period. The time period for Earth's orbit is approximately $$365.25$$ days.

title: Converting Time Period to Seconds
text: Convert the time period from days to seconds to use in the velocity formula. Remember, $$1$$ day $$=$$ $$24$$ hours, $$1$$ hour $$=$$ $$60$$ minutes, and $$1$$ minute $$=$$ $$60$$ seconds.

title: Calculate Earth's Velocity
text: Using the formula velocity $$=$$ $$\frac{2\pi radius}{time}$$ period, calculate Earth's velocity. Use radius $$=$$ $$1.5\times{10}^{11}$$ $$m$$ and time period in seconds.

title: Calculate Earth's Linear Momentum
text: Now that you have Earth's velocity, use the formula momentum $$=$$ mass * velocity to find Earth's linear momentum. Use mass $$=$$ $$5.97\times{10}^{24}$$ kg and velocity from the previous step.
